definition: The credit impulse (second derivative of private credit growth) is a critical leading indicator for business cycle turning points (Biggs, Mayer, Pick 2009) that the knowledge base tracks poorly. Government debt dynamics are extensively covered while this established academic indicator is ignored. High-value gap identification with zero corroboration within this iteration.
evidence: Well-supported in academic literature (Biggs, Mayer, Pick 2009)
Pair_2 confirmed as a genuine analytical blind spot
No other agent engages with it
No current credit impulse data provided
open_questions: What does the current credit impulse signal for the business cycle?
How does the credit impulse interact with the Kalecki profit channel?
